title: Crispy Tunisian Flour Flatbread
description: A traditional Tunisian dish featuring strong white flour and bhuna, this crispy flatbread is both bitter and tender, perfect for showing off your culinary skills. It is ovo-vegetarian friendly and can be served as a snack or side dish.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the strong white flour, salt, sugar, and bhuna.
Gradually add the olive oil, egg, and water to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4-6 equal pieces, depending on the desired size of the flatbread.
Roll out each piece of dough into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium-high heat and cook the flatbread for 1-2 minutes on each side, until crispy and golden brown.
Brush the flatbread with olive oil and sprinkle with sesame seeds, if desired.
Serve the crispy Tunisian flour flatbread hot, either on its own or with your favorite dips and spreads.
